hook.text: Whales are not just charismatic giants—they are ecosystem engineers of the open ocean.

buildup.text: When whales dive and surface, they push nutrients upward in plumes called 'whale pumps.'

discovery.text: Their iron-rich feces fertilize phytoplankton blooms that absorb carbon and produce oxygen.

twist.text: When a whale dies, its carcass feeds deep-sea communities for decades—a 'whale fall.'

climax.text: Whaling removed millions of whales and weakened nutrient cycling across entire oceans.

punchline.text: Whales fertilize the sea just by existing.

quiz.question: What is a 'whale pump'?
options: Nutrient movement from whale diving and surfacing
A machine used in whale research
A type of ocean current near whales
